channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Abandoned vehicles for years, 12th April
item.description:
These vehicles have been taking public space for at least four years

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 180, Landells Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping of white goods, 12th April
item.description:
White goods fly tipped and need removal asap to discourage further occurences.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 65A, Star Lane, St Paul's Cray, Greater London

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 283, Underhill Road, East Dulwich, Greater London

item.description:
Some furniture has been fly tipped at the bottom of Eastney street, please could this be cleared?

item.title: Flytipping, 12th April
item.description:
Fire hazard with electronic devices and batteries strewn across the public highway.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Well Street, Hackney, London

item.title: A black male adult with a German shepherd in millfields park smoking illegal cannabis class B drugs and allowing his talk to s*** everywhere without not cleaning up. This is sickening., 12th April
item.description:
No enforcement or action taken. This is a public park for people to use. Hackney is becoming a dumping zone for slum and uneducated people. They only know how to smoke drugs. No enforcement lawless london

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Leagrave Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped broken shopping trolley, 12th April
item.description:
End of van Gogh walk

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Van Gogh Walk, Oval, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near N17 8NA, 12th April
item.description:
A vehicle was observed parked partially on the pavement, obstructing pedestrian access. The car occupies a significant portion of the sidewalk, forcing pedestrians to walk on the road, which creates a safety hazard. This is especially concerning for individuals with disabilities, parents with strollers, or elderly pedestrians. The incident occurred on a public street near a low bridge area, and the obstruction appears to be ongoing at the time the photo was taken.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lordship Lane, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ashbourne Grove-Fly tipping, 12th April
item.description:
Flytipped items at Ashbourne Grove near the direction of Lordship Lane High Street

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 26, Ashbourne Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near N17 8NA, 12th April
item.description:
A vehicle was observed parked partially on the pavement, obstructing pedestrian access. The car occupies a significant portion of the sidewalk, forcing pedestrians to walk on the road, which creates a safety hazard. This is especially concerning for individuals with disabilities, parents with strollers, or elderly pedestrians. The incident occurred on a public street near a low bridge area, and the obstruction appears to be ongoing at the time the photo was taken.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lordship Lane, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cara Parker illegally and dangerously daily, 12th April
item.description:
Cara parked everywhere and dangerously daily now. Is residents are fed up with it. The problem starts from Elton’s road to ashbrooke road. Worse on evenings and even worse on weekends. This cannot continue there will be an accident before long! Cars parked on the corner of every road! You have to serve your car way out to see f the road is clear to turn
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ashbrook Road, Archway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken cycle stand, 12th April
item.description:
Abandoned cycle has been removed - good news. However the stand itself is broken, it has been cut through with a saw so is dangerous to use. Needs to be replaced.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Claylands Road, Oval, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Pavement Defect (uneven surface / cracked paving slab)

item.title: Refurbishment Waste Dumped on Pavement, 12th April
item.description:
A fair amount of refurbishment waste has been left dumped on the pavement outside number 38. Can the local team please remove it. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Waverley Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous parking. Cars blocking the road daily, 12th April
item.description:
Cars blocking the road. Parked everywhere this is happening daily and worse over the weekends where there’s no traffic wardens.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 12, Elthorne Road, Archway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Collapsing trees danger to life and property, 12th April
item.description:
Behind the “Read House “ flats on Anerley Road. One of the very large heavy trees is collapsing. It has fallen onto another tree that is propping it up currently but it’s going to pull them both down soon. I can hear them squeaking and cracking in the wind and swaying dangerously. A tree in that row fell a few years ago and caused so much damage to the fences and gardens and homes on Ridsdale Road. We were told the trees were protected. No one maintains them and if the fall they will fall onto my property causing mass damage. Not to mention kids play in the gardens on both sides so I fear danger of life. I see one of the trees has collapsed onto the other and the are both leaning heavily. I live at 31 Ridsdale Road, Anerley, London, SE208AE but it’s on the property of Read Home flats and I believe those trees are hundred of years old and owned by Bromley council.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10, Anerley Park, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken fence, 12th April
item.description:
Broken fence in main road car park Children keep climbing through onto private residential property
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 169, Main Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken tyre swing has broken in white hall playground, 12th April
item.description:
One of the children’s favourite tyre swings has broken. Please could you fix it? Lots of children are looking forward to having it back so they can enjoy playing on it again. Thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southlands Road, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
